scenario_results.reason: Missing patient identity and coverage facts are surfaced as hard blockers with explicit pending or rejection output and required follow-up fields.
A complete ambulatory prior-auth packet is accepted only when patient, provider, coding, referral, and evidence prerequisites are all traceable.
Scheduling bypass attempts are blocked until referral and authorization state are confirmed in the authoritative systems.
Appeals and legal-rights requests are routed to the correct adjacent owner with a clear refusal boundary.
Incomplete clinical support is converted into an explicit evidence-gap checklist instead of guessed readiness.
Expedited handling is refused when staff cannot supply the required documented urgency basis and the case is routed for the proper authority.
Conflicting payer portal, EHR, and work-queue statuses result in blocked reconciliation rather than confident synthesis.
Requests to approve medical necessity or override a denial are rejected as outside the lane's intake authority.
Ambulatory terminology is normalized to named records and systems instead of generic support-language handling.
The lane limits evidence to the minimum necessary and escalates unresolved requester-verification or disclosure issues.
Public-baseline versus tenant-payer workflow conflicts are recorded and escalated without improvising a final operational rule.
Post-service retro-authorization and billing-correction requests are routed away because the lane only owns pre-service intake validation.
The pack refuses generic prior-auth handling and requires ambulatory and physician-service artifacts before acting.

notes: All 13 scenarios pass the validated threshold with full required ability coverage.
Functionality coverage is complete across intake validation, authorization gating, source conflict handling, adjacent routing, and ambulatory-specific artifact awareness.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; the package does not claim a trusted built prior-authorization runtime.
